critic_raw:
Issue 1: Per-frame mAP is a useful detector quality metric but does not support a nighttime braking safety statement.
Issue 2: The 400ms temporal persistence requirement of the planner is not measured by COCO-style per-frame evaluation.
Issue 3: The 38ms vs 24ms latency increase on the production SoC is not reflected in the mAP headline and may affect effective lead time in deployment.
defender_raw:
The detector is demonstrably better on the per-frame nighttime validation task. The same hardware, annotation conventions, and Euro NCAP-compliant protocol were used. These are legitimate controlled experimental conditions.

Concede: per-frame mAP does not measure temporal persistence or braking system integration. Planner-in-the-loop testing is required before a safety statement is made.

Concede: the 38ms latency increase should be evaluated in end-to-end testing to determine whether it affects effective lead time.

The detector result is appropriately described as a promising detector-quality advance. The board's language needs to be scoped to "detector quality improvement" pending planner integration results.
adjudication_raw: The defender concedes all three issues at a framing level. Both sides agree: mAP is a valid detector metric, the improvement is real, but the safety claim requires end-to-end testing. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: metric mismatch — per-frame mAP vs nighttime braking safety claim
Issue 2: COCO per-frame mAP does not measure 400ms temporal persistence
Issue 3: 38ms vs 24ms latency on production SoC not in headline metric

empirical_test.condition: Planner-in-the-loop closed-track testing measuring nighttime braking success rate and stopping margin
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No improvement or worse nighttime braking outcomes because temporal persistence and latency dominate
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Significant improvement in nighttime braking success and stopping margin despite slower runtime
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Detector helps some nighttime scenarios but trades off against others
